# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent memcached exposed port.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1  # API version of the deployment, in this case v1 (apps) and kind(kinds are used for creating new objects like pods etc.)  
kind: Deployment    # Kind is a type that Kubernetes uses to create an object. In our scenario it'll be 'Deployment'. 
metadata:            # Metadata contains information about the deployment, such as name and namespace of your application stack in k8s cluster (like app=myapp)  
  labels:           # Label is a way for Kubernetes to identify which pod(s)/container(s). In our case it'll be 'purpose = web', because we are using this deployment type as the main entry point of your application.   
      purpose: web    
  name: myapp-web   # Name will give a unique identifier for each object in Kubernetes cluster (like app=myapp) and can also serve to identify resource when used with kubectl commands like get, describe etc., e.g 'kubectl get pods' would return all the web application related Pod(s).
spec:              # Spec is a set of instructions for how Kubernetes should create or update your object (like number and type of containers to run in this case)  
  replicas: 3       # Replicaset controls replication factor. In our scenario, we want the web application pod(s), memcached cache service & background workers deployment have three instances running at any given time   
  selector:         # Selector is a way for Kubernetes to know which Pod should be used as primary entry point of your app (like purpose=web)  
      matchLabels:    
          purpose: web      
  template:        # Template describes how the pod(s)/container will look like when created by this deployment. In our case, it'll have one container that runs a specific image and has environment variables set for your app (like PORT=3001).  
      metadata:         
         labels:          
            purpose: web       # Label is used to identify which pod(s) should be controlled by this template. In our case, it'll match with 'purpose = web'.   
      spec:              # Spec describes the container that will run in Pod (like image name and environment variables).  
         containers:     # Containers are a set of one or more instances running on each pod(s) 
            - name: myapp-web       # Name is used to identify this particular instance. In our case, it'll be 'myapp-web'.   
              image: your_image   # Image specifies the container should run with a specific Docker image (like nginx or nodejs).  This can also include version if needed for multi stage builds etc., e.g mynodeversion/imagename and command to start app in case of docker images, like "npm install && nodemon your_app".
              env:               # Environment variables are set inside the container (like PORT=3001)  
                 - name: PORT     # Name is used for identifying this particular environment variable. In our scenario it'll be 'PORT'.   
                   value: "3002"  # Value sets what should happen when a Pod or Container with the specified label(s), and its associated Environment Variable, are started (like PORT=3001).   This can also include other environment variables. e.g - name = DB_HOST value =  "dbserver".
